descriptions: The Foriglio and Ladini were targeted together in the same operation, indicating association.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 15 dicembre 2015 vengono eseguiti 36 fermi, di cui 8 arresti, dalle forze dell'ordine che concludono l'operazione Saggio Compagno per traffico di stupefacenti, estorsione, porto d'armi illecito e associazione mafiosa contro presunti affiliati ai Petullà, i Foriglio e i Ladini.

descriptions: The Petullà and Ladini were targeted together in the same operation, indicating association.

Alameddine crime network.description: Australian organised crime group based in Merrylands, Western Sydney, led by Rafat Alameddine. Allegedly one of Sydney's largest drug-trafficking organisations, earning around $1 million weekly profit at its peak. Involved in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family during the Sydney gangland war of the early 2020s. Some members have links to Islamic extremism.

descriptions: The friendly relationship between Bilal Alameddine and the Ahmads ended.
edges.evidence_quote: the friendliness between Bilal Alameddine and the Ahmads had, at latest, came to an end by then- if it hadn't already years prior.

descriptions: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud Ahmad, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud 'Brownie' Ahmad

descriptions: Talal Alameddine brawled with Bassam Hamzy, founder of Brothers for Life.
edges.evidence_quote: Talal Alameddine got into a brawl with fellow Goulburn Correctional Centre inmate Bassam Hamzy, founder of the Brothers for Life organisation.

descriptions: The Alameddines are in a feud with the Hamzy crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family

descriptions: Alameddine and Hamze families are rival criminal families in Australia engaged in feuds.
edges.evidence_quote: Often different rival criminal families are interlocked in feuds (e.g. Alameddine VS Hamze), resulting in a fair amount of violence.

descriptions: Alameddine crime network and Brothers for Life are in a feud resulting in shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: As of 2024, the ongoing feud between the Alameddine crime network and the Brothers for Life of the Hamzy family has led to at least 11 shooting deaths.

descriptions: The Comancheros are close allies and business partners of the Alameddines.
edges.evidence_quote: The Comanchero Motorcycle Club, who were close allies and business partners of the Alameddines through Mohammad 'Almo' Alameddine

descriptions: The Alameddines and Elmirs are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: This suggests that the Alameddines and Elmirs are still allies.

descriptions: The Alameddines are in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family

descriptions: The two groups are engaged in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family

descriptions: Rafat Alameddine learned from Bilal Haouchar, indicating a mentorship relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Rafat Alameddine spent time as a younger man shadowing Bilal Haouchar, and learning the criminal trade under his guidance.

descriptions: The Hauochars are long-time allies of the Alameddine network.
edges.evidence_quote: Alameddine's long-time allies, the Hauochars

descriptions: Talal Alameddine implied a connection between the network and ISIS.
edges.evidence_quote: During legal proceedings, Talal Alameddine implied the network having a connection to the Islamic State (ISIS) militant group

descriptions: The text reports that in-fighting began between the two groups in 2025, indicating a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2025, The Daily Telegraph reported that in-fighting had allegedly began between the Alameddine family and the KVT street gang, their long-time ally.

descriptions: KVT is used as muscle by the Alameddines.
edges.evidence_quote: Police sources stated that Vokai was viewed as the leader of the infamous Islander gang KVT, who have regularly been used as muscle by the Alameddines.

descriptions: Associates of both networks jointly attempted a robbery.
edges.evidence_quote: On 29 November 2024, associates of the Alameddine and KVT crime networks attempted to rob a tobacco delivery truck in Guildford.

descriptions: The text states that KVT was a long-time ally of the Alameddine family, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2025, The Daily Telegraph reported that in-fighting had allegedly began between the Alameddine family and the KVT street gang, their long-time ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Ali 'Ay Huncho' Younes got into a fight with ONEFOUR associate Sesita 'Radistarz' Lyzwa

descriptions: R4W is a subset of the Alameddine crime network.
edges.evidence_quote: The men arrested were part of a subset within the Alameddine crime network known as 'R4W', or Ready for War.

descriptions: Brothers for Life was in conflict with the Alameddine crime network from 2020 to 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: Between 2020 and 2022, the gang was again in the public eye as one side of a new conflict, then against the Alameddine crime network.

descriptions: Tensions between Brothers for Life and Alameddine crime network continued in 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: On 18 December 2024, The Daily Telegraph released an article detailing a bizarre jail call between an accused Melbourne gunman, and an unknown associate of the Alameddine crime network, demonstrating that tensions between the crews were ongoing and had even gone interstate.

Hongmen.description: The Hongmen, formerly the Tiandihui, is a Chinese fraternal organization founded in 1761 in Fujian, Qing dynasty. Historically a secretive folk religious sect with Ming loyalist and anti-Qing roots, it spread globally and became known as Chinese Freemasons. In modern times, some branches have been associated with criminal activity, though the current iteration is secular.

descriptions: Ghee Hin was a constituent part of the larger Hongmen network.
edges.evidence_quote: was part of the Hongmen overseas network.

descriptions: Chee Kung Tong was established by the Tiandihui.
edges.evidence_quote: One of the first tongs was established by the secret society Chee Kung Tong, which aimed to restore the power of the Ming dynasty by removing the new Manchu rulers of the Qing dynasty.

descriptions: Hongmen groups turned to crime and gave birth to the modern Triads.
edges.evidence_quote: When some Hongmen groups based within China could no longer rely on donations from sympathetic locals; being unable to resume normal civilian lives after years of hiding, they turned to illegal activities – thus giving birth to the modern Triads.

descriptions: Tiandihui is descended from the White Lotus Sect.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tiandihui's ancestral organisation

descriptions: Hongmen's predecessor Tiandihui is described as being in the vein of White Lotus Society, which is its ancestral organization.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hongmen (Vast Family), formerly Tiandihui (Heaven and Earth Society), is a Chinese fraternal organisation and historically a secretive folk religious sect in the vein of the Ming loyalist and anti-Qing White Lotus Society, the Tiandihui's ancestral organisation.

descriptions: Triad originated from the Tiandihui.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tiandihui, the Heaven and Earth Society, also called Hongmen...

descriptions: Kuntsevskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang

descriptions: Bandidos and Homietos were in a deadly conflict leading to retaliatory shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2023, three Bandidos members were shot and killed in several locations along I-45 while traveling to a funeral in Oklahoma City. The funeral was the result of a deadly conflict that had occurred there earlier that month with the rival gang Homietos.

Terceiro Comando Puro.description: Terceiro Comando Puro (TCP) is a Brazilian criminal organization based in Rio de Janeiro, founded on February 25, 2002, as a dissident faction of the Terceiro Comando. It is the second largest criminal faction in Rio de Janeiro and the third in Brazil, after PCC and Comando Vermelho. The group is involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and murders, and operates in multiple Brazilian states. It is currently active and rivals the Comando Vermelho and Amigos dos Amigos.

descriptions: ADA and Terceiro Comando Puro are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang's main rivals are Comando Vermelho and Terceiro Comando Puro.

Muttahida Qaumi Movement – London.description: A Muhajir nationalist and secular political party in Pakistan, founded by Altaf Hussain in 1984. It was the dominant political force in Karachi and a key coalition partner in Pakistan's federal government. After a 2016 crackdown, the party became inactive and was officially dissolved in 2025.

descriptions: APMSO evolved into MQM, making APMSO the predecessor.
edges.evidence_quote: APMSO gave birth to the Muhajir Qaumi Movement in 1984.

descriptions: APMSO was the student organization that founded MQM.

descriptions: MQM-Haqiqi is a splinter faction that separated from MQM.

edges.evidence_quote: the separate MQM–Pakistan was run by Khalid Maqbool Siddiqui based in Pakistan

edges.evidence_quote: Younger Younger Ghetto Boys (YYGB)

Felony Lane Gang.description: The Felony Lane Gang is an organized crime gang based in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, United States. They specialize in smash-and-grab thefts from parked cars to steal identification, which they use to cash fraudulent checks at drive-through banks, often using the lane furthest from surveillance cameras. The gang recruits drug addicts and others in desperate situations to commit these crimes, and they operate nationwide. Leadership has been identified, with Travis J. Russ sentenced to 188 months in prison in 2014 for leading the gang.

edges.target: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland
descriptions: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland is a KKK-related group.
edges.evidence_quote: German authorities conducted raids against a group called the National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland

Clan Abbinante.description: The Clan Abbinante is a Camorra organization operating in the northern area of Naples, specifically in Marano di Napoli. Founded by Raffaele Abbinante, it was originally affiliated with the Nuvoletta clan and later became a trusted group of Paolo Di Lauro. The clan is involved in drug trafficking and cigarette smuggling, and has been engaged in violent feuds with rival clans. Key members include Raffaele, Guido, and Antonio Abbinante.

descriptions: The Abbinante clan was in a feud with the rival Di Lauro clan, involving many murders.
edges.evidence_quote: è il mandante di uno dei cinquanta e più omicidi che hanno insanguinato la città durante la faida con il clan rivale Di Lauro

descriptions: The Abbinante brothers were top members of the Di Lauro clan.
edges.evidence_quote: erano ritenuti dagli investigatori elementi di vertice del clan di spacciatori capeggiato da Paolo Di Lauro

descriptions: Raffaele Abbinante was originally an affiliate of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Abbinante, detto “Papele e Marano”, nasce come affiliato al clan Nuvoletta

descriptions: Francesco Abbinante sided with the Scissionisti, a splinter group.
edges.evidence_quote: prima di schierarsi con gli Scissionisti

descriptions: Abbinante clan boss was aligned with Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian Direzione Distrettuale Antimafia first investigated the clan in 2002, which led to the imprisonment of its most influential members, including Abbinante clan boss Raffaele 'Papale e Marano' Abbinante, who was aligned with Di Lauro.

descriptions: Licciardi and Abbinante clans coordinated in a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing, reportedly orchestrated by both the Licciardi and Sautto-Ciccarelli clans in coordination with the Abbinante clan, was interpreted by prosecutors as both an act of strategic retribution and an assertion of territorial dominance.

descriptions: Sautto-Ciccarelli and Abbinante clans coordinated in a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing, reportedly orchestrated by both the Licciardi and Sautto-Ciccarelli clans in coordination with the Abbinante clan, was interpreted by prosecutors as both an act of strategic retribution and an assertion of territorial dominance.

descriptions: Redliners were a puppet club indirectly controlled by Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: As a puppet club of a puppet club, the police reported the 'triple insulation' made it difficult to tie Stadnick to the Redliners.

descriptions: Redliners merged into los Brovos.
edges.evidence_quote: Shortly afterwards, the Redliners joined los Bravos, which increased Stadnick's influence in los Bravos.

descriptions: Rockers established the Redliners as a puppet club in Winnipeg.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Stadnick had the Angels' puppet club in Montreal, the Rockers, set up a puppet club in Winnipeg called the Redliners.

descriptions: Rockers MC founded the Redliners as an auxiliary club.
edges.evidence_quote: Walter "Nurget" Stadnick instructed the Rockers Motor Club to found an auxiliary outlaw motorcycle club of their own which would be based out of out west in Winnipeg, Manitoba . The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners

descriptions: The Shimizu family sold its emblem to the head of Goryo-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Tanabe sold the Shimizu emblem for circa tens of millions of yen to Yasuo Takagi (head of the Goryo-kai and thereafter the Mio-gumi gang).

descriptions: The Mio-gumi sought to acquire the Shimizu family crest.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mio-gumi wanted the upscale Shimizu crest to recover from their billions of yen in loan-shark losses when Takagi was arrested in 2003.

Corsican mafia.description: The Mafia corsa refers to criminal organizations originating from Corsica, with a tradition of banditry and violence. It operates primarily in Corsica, Marseille, and the Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur region, with extensions to North Africa, the Antilles, and South America. The groups are numerous, often small, and involved in extortion, drug trafficking (notably the French Connection heroin network), and high rates of homicide. The most prominent recent group is the Petit Bar gang of Ajaccio.

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the Corsican mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... African-American mob , Armenian mafia , Chaldean mafia , Corsican mafia , Dixie Mafia , Greek mafia , Jewish-American mob , Lebanese mafia , Mexican Mafia , Russian mafia , Serbian mafia , various motorcycle gangs (such as the Hells Angels , Outlaws , and Pagan's ) , various street gangs (such as the Bloods , Crips , and Latin Kings )

Chambers Brothers.description: The Chambers Brothers were a criminal organization based in Detroit, Michigan, heavily involved in crack cocaine distribution during the mid-1980s. At their peak, they controlled over 50% of Detroit's crack trade, operating around 700 crack houses and generating $3 million daily. The gang was founded by four brothers from Marianna, Arkansas: Larry, Billy Joe, Willie Lee, and Otis Chambers. They were dismantled after a joint DEA and Detroit Police investigation led to their arrest in 1988 and conviction in 1989.

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are mentioned alongside Chambers Brothers as comparable Detroit gangs, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Like other Detroit street gangs, such as their Westside Detroit counterparts in the late 1970s; the Nasty Flynns (later the NF Bangers), and 7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs or the drug consortiums of the 1980s such as Young Boys Inc., Pony Down, Best Friends, Black Mafia Family and the Chambers Brothers, the Errol Flynns grew out of the racial and economic unrest that transformed Detroit in the late 1960s and 1970s.

descriptions: The Chambers Brothers rose to prominence after YBI's downfall, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Many gangs, such as "Best Friends", "Pony Down", " Black Mafia Family ", and " The Chambers Brothers ", rose to prominence in the wake of the YBI
